question_text: A 5 kg box is pushed across a horizontal surface with a force of 20 N. Calculate the acceleration of the box, assuming friction is negligible. (Use F = ma)
explanation: Using F = ma, we can rearrange to find a = F/m. Acceleration = 20 N ÷ 5 kg = 4 m/s². This shows how different forces produce different accelerations depending on the mass.
